introduction.title: Vietnamese Conditionals (If-Then)
content: Vietnamese conditionals follow a simple 'if X, then Y' structure: 'nếu X, thì Y'. Both 'nếu' (if) and 'thì' (then) are common, though 'thì' is often dropped in casual speech.
Unlike English, Vietnamese verbs don't change form for hypothetical situations — there's no past subjunctive ('if I were'). The same words handle real, hypothetical, and counterfactual conditionals.
Other conditional connectors include 'khi' (when), 'trừ khi' (unless), and 'miễn là' (as long as).

sections.title: If ... Then
sections.description: The basic conditional pattern — nếu ... thì

notes: Placed at the beginning of the condition clause.
Works for both real and hypothetical 'if' situations.
'Nếu như' is more emphatic — adds weight to the condition.
Can be omitted in casual speech if context is clear.
Placed at the start of the result clause.
Often omitted in casual speech — more common to drop than 'nếu'.
Also used as a general topic-marker outside conditionals.

examples.vietnamese: Nếu trời mưa thì tôi ở nhà
examples.english: If it rains, then I'll stay home
examples.literalEnglish: If sky rain then I stay home

examples.vietnamese: Nếu bạn muốn, tôi sẽ giúp
examples.english: If you want, I will help
examples.literalEnglish: If you want I will help
examples.notes: 'thì' omitted — very common in spoken Vietnamese

examples.vietnamese: Nếu như bạn cố gắng thì sẽ thành công
examples.english: If you really try, you will succeed
examples.literalEnglish: If-emphatic you try then will succeed
examples.notes: 'Nếu như' adds more weight than plain 'nếu'

examples.vietnamese: Trời mưa thì tôi không đi
examples.english: If it rains, I won't go
examples.literalEnglish: Weather rain then I not go
examples.notes: 'nếu' omitted — context makes the condition clear

examples.vietnamese: Nếu tôi biết thì tôi đã giúp bạn
examples.english: If I had known, I would have helped you
examples.literalEnglish: If I know then I already help you
examples.notes: Counterfactual: 'đã' (already) in the result implies it would have happened — but didn't

sections.title: When (Time-Based)
sections.description: Conditional time references — 'when X happens, Y'

notes: Can refer to past, present, or future — context decides.
Often paired with 'thì' in the result clause.
Literally 'when' + 'any' — used for unspecified or future 'when' situations.
Also used as a question word.

examples.vietnamese: Khi tôi đến, hãy gọi tôi
examples.english: When I arrive, please call me

examples.vietnamese: Khi nào rảnh, tôi sẽ gọi

sections.title: Other Conditional Words
sections.description: Unless, as long as, suppose, in case, if only

notes: Means 'except when' — introduces the only exception.
Equivalent to English 'unless'.
Literally 'except when'.
Literally 'exempt' + 'is' — sets a condition that must be met.
Often used to give permission with a constraint.
Literally 'pretend' + 'assume' — introduces hypothetical scenarios.
More formal — often for imagining possibilities.

trong trường hợp.meaning: In case / in the event that
notes: Literally 'in' + 'case' — used for contingency planning.
Common in official or professional contexts.

chỉ cần.meaning: If only / you just need to
notes: Emphasizes the condition is simple or minimal.
Literally 'only need' — reassuring tone.

examples.vietnamese: Tôi sẽ đi trừ khi trời mưa
examples.english: I will go unless it rains
examples.literalEnglish: I will go unless weather rain

examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể đi miễn là về sớm
examples.english: You can go as long as you come back early
examples.literalEnglish: You can go as-long-as return early

examples.vietnamese: Giả sử bạn là tôi, bạn sẽ làm gì?
examples.english: Suppose you were me, what would you do?
examples.literalEnglish: Suppose you are me, you will do what

examples.vietnamese: Trong trường hợp khẩn cấp, hãy gọi 115
Chỉ cần bạn cố gắng thì sẽ ổn
examples.english: If you just try, it'll be fine
examples.literalEnglish: Only need you try then will fine
